items.hook: Using AI for creative pursuits? Moderation is key
items.body: An experiment showed AI users had the most creative ideas when they used it in moderation – not too much and not too little. Columnist David Robson puts the finding to the test, and explores what we lose when we over-rely on AI

items.hook: The Romans is the fourth serial of the second season of the British science fiction television series Doctor Who.
items.body: Written by Dennis Spooner and directed by Christopher Barry, the serial was broadcast on BBC1 in four weekly parts from 16 January to 6 February 1965. The world is stranger than it lets on.

items.hook: ‘Colony’ director Yeon Sang-ho talks sequel plans through novel, video game
items.body: EXCLUSIVE: The film has proved a box office hit in South Korea and is set for a US release on August 28.

Experts believe it’s at least possible. We urgently need a plan to navigate the ethical implications

items.hook: The film premiered at the 51st Semaine de la Critique at the 2012 Cannes Film Festival, where it won the inaugural France 4 Visionary Award.
items.body: Sofia's Last Ambulance is a feature-length observational documentary film by Bulgarian director Ilian Metev. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.hook: Bruno Mars has an unexpected opening before Wembley party starts
items.body: After an eight-year absence from UK stages, Bruno Mars returned to Wembley Stadium with a polished, hit-filled show.

items.hook: Bayeux Tapestry in 'wonderful state' after journey to London, French minister says
items.body: The French culture minister has told the BBC that the Bayeux Tapestry is in "a wonderful state".

items.hook: Orphans of Chaos is a 2005 science fantasy novel by John C.
items.body: It is the first volume of the Orphans of Chaos trilogy that continues with the novels Fugitives of Chaos (2006) and Titans of Chaos (2007). Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit hole.

items.hook: ESA’s Mars Express meets NASA’s Psyche

items.body:
Actor who was the first Irish woman to win an Oscar for her role in the 1989 film classic My Left Foot

Brenda Fricker, who has died aged 81, was only the second Irish actor – and the first female one – to win an Oscar, for her role

items.hook: Shuggie Bain is the debut novel by Scottish-American writer Douglas Stuart, published in 2020.
items.body: It tells the story of the youngest of three children, Shuggie, growing up with his alcoholic mother Agnes in 1980s post-industrial working-class Glasgow, Scotland. Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit hole.

items.opener: Here's one for the group chat —
items.hook: Geek Girl is the debut novel by Holly Smale, published in 2013.
items.body: It won a 2014 Waterstones Children's Book Prize prize, along with a 2014 Leeds Book Award. Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.

items.hook: Justinian (ISBN 0-8125-4527-3), was published in 1998 by Tor Books.
items.body: It is a novel by American writer Harry Turtledove writing under the pseudonym H. Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.
